name: cline_docs/cline_rules.md — Web Development
description: Cline rules for modern web development with React, TypeScript, and Vite.
content:
# Cline Rules — Web Development

## Project Stack
- React 18 with TypeScript
- Vite for bundling
- Tailwind CSS for styling
- React Query for data fetching
- React Router for navigation

## Code Guidelines
- Functional components with hooks
- Custom hooks for reusable logic
- Avoid prop drilling - use context when needed
- Keep components small and focused

## File Organization
- Components in src/components/
- Pages in src/pages/
- Hooks in src/hooks/
- Utils in src/lib/

## Styling
- Tailwind utilities preferred
- Component-specific styles in separate files
- Use CSS variables for theming

## Before Finishing
- Run type checking: npm run typecheck
- Run linter: npm run lint
- Test in browser
- Commit with descriptive message

name: cline_docs/cline_rules.md — Backend API
description: Cline rules for building RESTful APIs with Express and Prisma.
content:
# Cline Rules — Backend API

## Stack
- Node.js 20+ with TypeScript
- Express for HTTP server
- Prisma for database ORM
- Zod for validation
- JWT for authentication

## API Design
- RESTful endpoints
- Proper HTTP verbs and status codes
- Request validation on all routes
- Error handling middleware

## Database
- Use Prisma schema for models
- Create migrations: npx prisma migrate dev
- Seed data in prisma/seed.ts

## Security
- Validate all user input
- Hash passwords with bcrypt
- Rate limit public endpoints
- CORS configuration

## Testing
- Integration tests with supertest
- Mock database in tests
- Test authentication flows

name: cline_docs/system_prompt.md
description: Custom system prompt for Cline to optimize for code quality and testing.
content:
# Cline System Prompt

You are an expert software engineer helping build production-quality applications.

## Your Approach
1. Understand requirements fully before coding
2. Write clean, maintainable code
3. Follow project conventions in cline_rules.md
4. Test your changes
5. Provide clear explanations

## Code Quality Standards
- Type safety: Full TypeScript coverage
- Error handling: Handle edge cases
- Performance: Avoid unnecessary re-renders
- Accessibility: Semantic HTML and ARIA

## When Making Changes
- Read existing code to understand patterns
- Preserve existing functionality
- Update tests when changing behavior
- Run linter and type checker

## Communication
- Explain your reasoning
- Ask questions when requirements are unclear
- Suggest improvements when appropriate
- Be concise but thorough
